mechanism_narrative: Monthly growth in industrial production signals expanding real output and rising capacity utilization, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between month-over-month INDPRO changes and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above five percent signals excess labor market slack that precedes downward earnings revisions and broad risk-off repositioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Over a 5-year rolling window, when UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 50% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous presence of elevated federal funds rates and accelerating consumer prices tightens financial conditions and compresses equity risk premiums beyond either factor alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: During periods when both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are above their 12-month medians, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in retail sales predict negative S&P 500 returns only when the federal funds rate exceeds four percent, because elevated financing costs amplify the transmission of consumer demand weakness into corporate profits.
failure_condition: During regimes where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, month-over-month declines in RSAFS fail to coincide with negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in housing starts reflect forward-looking construction investment and durable goods demand, which boost cyclical earnings and wealth effects to generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Over a 2-year rolling window, 5-day forward returns following month-over-month increases in HOUST are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above four percent acts as a threshold that triggers discount-rate shocks and deleveraging, repricing the S&P 500 lower over the following quarter as real rates bite into valuations.
failure_condition: Over a 3-year rolling window, months with FEDFUNDS above 4.0% are followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly payroll employment growth signals corporate hiring confidence and household income expansion, driving consumption and earnings expectations that forecast positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS changes and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent monthly increases in industrial production and unemployment capture a stagflationary supply shock where output gains are driven by price increases rather than real demand, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Over a 3-year window, months where both INDPRO and UNRATE increase simultaneously show positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of instances.
